task_updates.executor_notes: Extended the payment DB handler surface with guild-scoped wallet, route, and payment helpers; tightened state transitions so retry stays failed-only, release stays the only path out of manual_hold, and wallet verification is cleared when a verified address changes; and pushed recovery/pending-confirmation fetches into filtered guild-scoped Supabase queries. Verified with compileall plus the full `tests/test_social_publications.py` and `tests/test_social_publish_service.py` modules.

Completed the reusable payments provider package by defining a typed `SendResult` contract, exporting the payment provider surface, and keeping `SolanaProvider` as a thin adapter over the existing `SolanaClient` with explicit `pre_submit`, `submitted`, and `ambiguous` send outcomes plus confirmation fallback checks. Verified with compileall plus the full `tests/test_social_publications.py` and `tests/test_social_publish_service.py` modules.

sense_check_acknowledgments.executor_note: Yes. The handler now covers wallet registry upsert/get/verify flows, payment-route CRUD plus list compatibility, payment creation/query/claim helpers, guarded state transitions, and guild-filtered recovery/pending-confirmation fetches while keeping retry blocked for submitted/manual_hold and release as the only exit from manual_hold.

Yes. The provider layer now exposes `SendResult` with explicit `pre_submit`/`submitted`/`ambiguous` phases, keeps confirmation and recovery status checks separate, and still wraps the in-memory `SolanaClient` directly without changing how the private key or keypair is loaded or stored.
